text: In Agile methodology, teams work in short development cycles called **sprints**, usually lasting one to four weeks. Each sprint begins with a planning meeting, where the team selects tasks from a **backlog**—a prioritized list of features and fixes. Tasks are often described as **user stories**, which explain a feature from the end-user’s perspective. To stay aligned, the team holds daily **standups**, brief meetings where members share progress and obstacles. Work is delivered incrementally through **iterative development**, meaning the team regularly releases small, functional updates and improves based on feedback. This approach helps teams adapt quickly to changes and deliver value faster.
